# GitHub as a possible tracking system for WP Credits students?

[Original Source](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1glocyFw0ATE4mUmYcx5ckPTdD2dy-f-MKOiCQkBx5Ig/edit?tab=t.0#heading=h.xs627fau743)

Thoughts (Celi): 

GitHub is a collaboration tool, to combine the work of many people in one common project. This is good for WordPress projects, but not the right scenario for a classroom with several students, each of them working on their own project and tasks. 

The only option that looks promising for tracking student progress in GitHub in my opinion is the option 1, GitHub classroom, but it has a major downside regarding how mentors can access the classroom (being owners in the GitHub organization?). 

## 

## **Option 1: GitHub classroom**

[https://classroom.github.com/classrooms](https://classroom.github.com/classrooms)

[https://docs.github.com/es/education/manage-coursework-with-github-classroom/get-started-with-github-classroom/about-github-classroom](https://docs.github.com/es/education/manage-coursework-with-github-classroom/get-started-with-github-classroom/about-github-classroom)

### Highlights and pros: 

* You can create 1 classroom per University/year/etc.   
* You can add manually the list of students to each classroom.  
* You can create individual assignments just once and then replicate them in other classrooms with one click.   
* To get the student enrolled in their assignments, you share with them a link per each assignment. 

### Requisites and possible problems: 

* The creator needs to have an organization (in principle we have it with WordPress)  
* I am not sure, but it looks like all teachers need to be owners in the GitHub organization to be mentors/admin in the classroom. This could be a real problem.  
* But there is the possibility to create a new organization (it could be called WP Credits) and have there only the repositories needed for this project, and give mentors ownership in that organization. Could this be an option?![][image1]

* I think it would be good to have a repository per each task/assigment we create. When creating a task (assignment), you assign a repository template to it that gets created and forked when the student enrolled on it. This means each student gets their own copy of the repository. Also a pull request is created in the moment that assignment is accepted by the students. That is the place to discuss with the mentor about the assignment, and where they can do changes to the files and ask for a revision. But this means creating several repositories, we will need admin access to the organization. ![][image2]  
* ![][image3]

## **Option 2: Repository \+ branch creation per every student**

* Each student would have to create a new branch and modify the documents we create in the repository (one document per task, more or less), and create a pull request to modify the documents with their progress.   
* This could be messy (we would need to control very well that they can create a branch but not commit an close a pull request).   
* If they cannot create a branch without the option of committing, we will have to create the branch for each them so they can work on it.   
* Having so many branches (each pull request creates a new one) could be messy for the mentors and students. 

## **![][image4]**

## **Option 3: Issues and sub-issues per student.** 

* Each student would have to copy from a document the tasks and create a new issue with their name.   
* Once done, they can create subissues from the tasks included in that issue. 

![][image5]

* Not sure how can the mentor control the issue status (on progress, done) and which are the permissions to close the issues and subissues that the students will have.   
* Everything will be discussed in the subissues and issues. 

# Alternative to GitHub

## **Create a website with LMS to track students**

* Sensei Pro and addons (sponsored by Auttomatic)  
* We can add teachers/mentors, grading, activities, etc.   
* We can still use slack as a communication channel outside between mentor and student.   
* We can use the website to introduce forms, slides pages (like a power point for pitches), etc. 

### Request: Sensei Pro license to support WP Credits pilot

We’ve kicked off the pilot of [**WordPress Credits**](https://wordpress.org/news/2025/07/introducing-wordpress-credits-a-new-contribution-internship-program-for-university-students/), a new contribution internship program connecting university students with WordPress project work. As part of the infrastructure, we’d like to use **Sensei Pro** (and relevant add-ons) to manage learning paths, student progress, mentor feedback, and grading.  
This program is supported by the WordPress Foundation and aims to scale globally. The pilot phase starts with the University of Pisa, with more universities already expressing interest. Having a solid LMS in place will help ensure a consistent and scalable contributor experience.

**Opportunities for Sensei:**

* Expanded visibility and usage across international academic programs  
* Early exposure to students who may become long-term WordPress contributors and educators  
* A feedback loop from real-world educational use cases that could inform future product improvements  
* A strong use case for Sensei as a learning tool inside open-source communities

We’d like to request a free Sensei Pro license and add-ons to support this phase, as we plan to centralize most learning materials and activities within a WordPress website that will use Sensei Pro as LMS.

Tagging \+quakep2 and @miroslavmitev to ask if it would also be possible to organize a walkthrough of Sensei for the program admins: one of us has used it in client work, but the rest of the team is new to using it for this kind of educational workflow.
